description:
# Why

bl tracks its internal `.balls/` runtime paths in two independent
hand-maintained lists:

- `review_safety::RUNTIME_PATHS` — drives the `bl review` squash
  backstop (`commit_touches_runtime`, `add_user_changes`). Currently
  `[.balls/local, .balls/tasks, .balls/worktree, .balls/code-refs]`.
- `store_init::ensure_main_gitignore`'s `wanted` list — drives what
  `bl init` writes to the project `.gitignore`. Currently
  `[.balls/local, .balls-worktrees, .balls/code-refs]` always, plus
  `[.balls/tasks, .balls/worktree]` when non-stealth.

The lists overlap but are not equal, and there is no mechanism keeping
them so — only a `// Kept in sync with ...` comment on RUNTIME_PATHS.
Every new `.balls/` sidecar has to be remembered in both places, with
the correct stealth gating in one of them. The track record shows the
cost of that: `.balls/code-refs` shipped without either entry and
needed two follow-up balls (bl-c4e2, bl-0b33) to retrofit; bl-ffb4's
`.balls/state-repo` is a third path with the same gap (bl-c439). Each
miss is a latent bug where `git add -A` can commit balls-internal
state — a clone of the task hub, a forge cache — into the project.

A manual-sync comment is a standing invitation to drift. This should
be one declaration that both consumers derive from.

# The shape of the fix (sketch — open to alternatives)

A single canonical table of runtime paths, each annotated with the one
piece of metadata that actually differs between the two consumers:
whether the path exists in stealth mode.

```
struct RuntimePath { rel: &'static str, in_stealth: bool }
// .balls/local      in_stealth: true
// .balls/tasks      in_stealth: false   (no state worktree in stealth)
// .balls/worktree   in_stealth: false
// .balls/code-refs  in_stealth: true
// .balls/state-repo in_stealth: true    (master_url is orthogonal to stealth)
```

Then:
- the squash backstop derives its path list from every entry (it is
  mode-independent — a path that never exists simply never matches);
- `ensure_main_gitignore` derives `wanted` by filtering on
  `in_stealth || !is_stealth`.

Adding a future sidecar becomes one table row, both consumers updated.
The `// Kept in sync` comment is deleted because the invariant is now
structural, not aspirational.

# Open questions for the implementer

- `.balls-worktrees` is gitignore-only today and lives *beside*
  `.balls/`, not inside it. It is arguably also a squash hazard (a
  committed `.balls-worktrees/<id>/` checkout is as bad as a committed
  `.balls/worktree`). Decide: fold it into the table as another entry,
  or keep it an explicit documented special case. Do not leave it
  ambiguous.
- Where the table lives: a small dedicated module reads cleanly given
  the 300-line cap and the two current call sites in different files.

# Scope

- Introduce the canonical table; rewrite `RUNTIME_PATHS` and
  `ensure_main_gitignore`'s `wanted` derivation to consume it.
- Delete the `Kept in sync with ...` comment.
- Pure refactor: no behavior change to the path *set* either consumer
  produces today (bl-c439 may add `.balls/state-repo` — coordinate so
  the set is correct whichever lands first). Existing tests in
  `review_safety_tests.rs`, `tests/review_safety.rs`, `tests/init*.rs`
  and `tests/coverage_write.rs` should pass unchanged; add coverage
  for the table-derivation if the 100% gate needs it.

# Origin

Flagged in the bl-c4e2 review pass: three `.balls/` runtime paths have
each needed separate retrofitting into both lists. The recurring cost,
not any single miss, is what this ball addresses.
title: Single source of truth for .balls runtime paths (gitignore + squash backstop)
